_note: LT10 — DCL counter-path OBSERVE-ONLY probe (LT10-A) + STATUS OUTPUT-CONTROL stage PROBE (LT10-B, log-only) on top of the full LT9 miss-output stack. LT10-A: a read-only ExecuteFirst hook at fn entry 0x30C798 (DclCounterPathProbeEnabled), the Strong-static (work/1783184308-dcl-miss-consumption-and-counter-path.md §Q2) candidate for the counter/reaction result-staging path that bypasses computeActionResult 0x309A44. At entry rcx = the staged result record; the managed drain logs one [DCL-CTRPATH] rcx=... targetIdx=... e8=... e9=... hp=... line per fire (bounded by DclCounterPathProbeMaxLogs=200). It writes NOTHING to game memory. PASS = provoke counters (attack units that have the Counter reaction) and confirm [DCL-CTRPATH] fires for the counter's target with sane values (targetIdx 0..63, hp matching the countering unit's, e8/e9 result bytes), correlating with the existing [DCL-MISS]/[DCL] reason=no-calc-entry lines from the same counter. ALSO OBSERVE whether [DCL-CTRPATH] fires for NORMAL attacks too — if it does, 0x30C798 is a SHARED post-roll commit, NOT counter-specific (REFUTES the hypothesis; valuable either way). [DCL-CTRPATH-SKIP] at install = AOB guard tripped, probe disabled, report immediately. --- LT10-B protocol (status output-control): DclStatusStageProbeEnabled=true is LOG-ONLY here — it dumps the staged status bytes ([DCL-STATUS] ail(+0x1A8)/mask(+0x1D0)/kind(+0x1C0)/resFlag(+0x1E5)) on every DCL-processed hit. STEP 1: run a proc attack (Venom Fang/Choco Beak-style, or a status spell) and read the [DCL-STATUS] lines to learn which byte the engine set on a proc hit vs a plain hit — this refines the Strong-static encoding. STEP 2 (between battles, settings edits ONLY, no rebuild): flip DclStatusSuppressEnabled=true to prove SUPPRESS (proc lands but no status appears); reset. STEP 3: set DclStatusForceId to an observed ailment id (and/or DclStatusForceValue+DclStatusForceOffset raw byte from STEP 1) to prove FORCE (plain basic attack inflicts the status). STEP 4: direct add/remove OUTSIDE actions — set StatusPokeTargetCharId + StatusPokeMode(add|remove) + StatusPokeOffset(0x1EF) + StatusPokeMask, one-shot ([STATUS-POKE] logged); and MovePokeTargetCharId + MovePokeValue to prove the Move +0x42 write ([MOVE-POKE] logged). The write modes stay OFF in this shipped profile; flip them per-iteration during the session. All LT9 control machinery (hit-control, miss output-control, kind flip) is left ON so the status observables from a forced-miss swing are also in play (LT9 open observable: does a forced-missed swing still stage a status?). --- LT10-C protocol (miss PRESENTATION): DclMissPresentationEnabled=true (kind 6, glyph bit 0x17) makes the forced-miss branch, in addition to zeroing the HP/MP debit, RMW the record's what-to-draw bitfield +0x1D8 (clear bit 2 = the damage-number route, set the glyph bit 0x17 = the evade/miss-glyph route) and write the glyph kind 0x06 to +0x1C0 and its mirror +0x360. GOAL: a forced miss should render as a Miss/evade glyph with NO damage number (case A: our write survives to the draw fn 0x2667E0). A/B TEST: if a '0'/number STILL shows on a forced miss (case B: the VM populates +0x1D8/+0x1C0 AFTER our pre-clamp hook at 0x30A66F and clobbers our write), RECORD it — the extended [DCL] line's pres= d8:0xOLD->0xNEW kind:0xOLD->0xNEW values tell where it got clobbered (if the logged NEW values look right but the screen shows a number, the clobber is downstream of our hook => the hook must move later; that is the next slice). ITERATE without a rebuild via settings edits between battles: if the glyph looks wrong, try DclMissPresentationGlyphBit 0x16 (the narrower class-Miss branch) vs 0x17 (generic), and DclMissPresentationKind 4 vs 6. Both the glyph-bit and kind mapping are Hypothesis (Strong-static 2026-07-04); the live run settles them.
_lt11_note: LT11 validates clean miss authorship at selector 0x205210 and selective suppression at all four native reaction Brave gates. The LT10 late presentation write, refuted counter-path probe, and status probe are disabled. See 1783912840-lt11-selector-reaction-gate-plan.md.
_lt12_note: LT12 validates formula-driven forecast hit-percent parity. DclPreviewHitPctEnabled mirrors the authored 50 from the same calc-entry decision used for execution into a native per-target buffer; the copy hook at 0x227FFE must show 50% instead of vanilla 100%. Fixed roll 90 makes the execution a deterministic miss after the forecast check.
